_meta.description: SYNTHETIC test fixture. Jason Statham is a fictional job applicant used to exercise the agent's job-application flow. Tailored to the FlashTec 'Senior .NET Engineer' posting (Dallas, TX). Do NOT use as real personal data.

school: The University of Texas at Dallas
summary: Senior .NET engineer with 9 years building high-throughput, low-latency payment and ledger systems in C# / .NET. Deep experience designing production ASP.NET Core Web APIs, modeling complex financial domains with Domain-Driven Design, and operating event-driven services on Azure. Comfortable owning features end-to-end from architecture through on-call, and mentoring mid-level engineers.
skills: Microsoft SQL Server (schema design, query tuning, transactions)
certifications: Microsoft Certified: Azure Solutions Architect Expert (AZ-305)

workHistory.title: Senior Software Engineer
highlights: Designed and operate a fleet of C# / .NET 8 microservices processing 3M+ card and ACH transactions per day at sub-100ms p99 latency.
Re-architected the core ledger service using Domain-Driven Design, cutting reconciliation defects by 70% and enabling daily settlement at scale.
Built event-driven pipelines on Azure Service Bus and Kafka for real-time fraud scoring and double-entry posting.
Tuned SQL Server schemas and queries, reducing settlement batch time from 45 minutes to under 6.
Mentor four mid-level engineers; introduced clean-architecture and automated-testing standards adopted team-wide.
Share the on-call rotation for a five-nines payments platform.

workHistory.title: Software Engineer II
highlights: Built and scaled ASP.NET Core Web APIs powering a B2B invoicing platform serving 12,000 merchants.
Migrated a legacy .NET Framework monolith to containerized .NET Core services deployed on Azure AKS.
Implemented gRPC inter-service contracts and high-performance serialization, halving internal call latency.
Established CI/CD pipelines in Azure DevOps with automated integration testing gates.

workHistory.title: Software Engineer
highlights: Developed C# / ASP.NET MVC features for client-facing logistics applications.
Designed normalized SQL Server schemas and authored tuned stored procedures.
Wrote xUnit test suites and helped raise team code-review standards.

education.degree: Bachelor of Science in Computer Science
education.school: The University of Texas at Dallas
education.details: GPA 3.7/4.0. Coursework in distributed systems, databases, and software engineering.

whyInterested: FlashTec's bet on .NET, Azure, and event-driven architecture to move real money at five-nines reliability is exactly the problem space I have spent my career in. I want to bring my payments and ledger experience to a Dallas-based platform team where I can own high-throughput services end-to-end and mentor the next generation of engineers.

voluntaryDisclosures.note: Synthetic placeholders only; left blank/decline as a real applicant might.
voluntaryDisclosures.veteranStatus: I am not a protected veteran
